metadata.title: Guideline on the clinical development of medicinal products intended for the treatment of chronic primary immune thrombocytopenia

rules.rule_text: Patients must have confirmed primary chronic ITP lasting more than 12 months since diagnosis.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients should have confirmed primary chronic ITP (lasting > 12 months since diagnosis)
rules.exceptions: This guideline does not cover primary immune thrombocytopenia of less than 12 months duration or secondary thrombocytopenia.

rules.rule_text: A full blood count should be normal except for the isolated thrombocytopenia. If patients with bleeding symptoms are included, haemoglobin must be at least above 9 g/dL.
rules.verbatim_quote: A full blood count should be normal except for the isolated thrombocytopenia. However, if patients with bleeding symptoms are included in the clinical studies a low haemoglobin level may be acceptable but should be at least above 9 g/dL.
rules.exceptions: Low haemoglobin acceptable if bleeding symptoms present, provided above 9 g/dL.

rules.rule_text: When anaemia due to bleeding is recorded, reticulocyte count must be measured to exclude reduced erythropoiesis by bone marrow impairment and a negative direct antiglobulin test (DAT) must be documented.
rules.verbatim_quote: If anaemia due to bleeding is recorded the reticulocyte count should be measured to exclude reduced erythropoiesis by bone marrow impairment and a negative direct antiglobulin test (DAT) be documented.

rules.rule_text: Screening for anti-nuclear antibodies (ANA) and anti-phospholipid antibodies (APLA) including anticardiolipin and lupus anticoagulant is required at baseline.
rules.verbatim_quote: Screening for anti-nuclear antibodies (ANA) and anti-phospholipid antibodies (APLA) including anticardiolipin and lupus anticoagulant will be required.
rules.exceptions: Patients with a positive test can be included provided they do not have any clinical manifestation of SLE or antiphospholipid syndrome.

rules.rule_text: ANA and APLA status should be tested at baseline and used as a covariant in a supportive analysis if an imbalance is found across treatment groups.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended to test ANA and APLA status at baseline and used as a covariant in a supportive analysis if an imbalance is found across treatment groups.

rules.rule_text: Exclusion criteria must apply to all causes of secondary ITP (e.g. viral infections, thyroid disease, autoimmune haemolytic anaemia). Normal quantitative immunoglobulin levels and a negative test for thyroglobulin should be recorded at baseline.
rules.verbatim_quote: Exclusion criteria for entering clinical studies apply to all causes of secondary ITP (e.g. viral infections, thyroid disease) or the presence of autoimmune haemolytic anaemia. Normal quantitative immunoglobulin (Ig) levels and a negative test for thyroglobulin should be recorded at baseline.

rules.rule_text: Patients with previous and recent history of thrombosis (arterial or venous) or significant risk factors for thrombosis must be excluded. A normal clotting screen at baseline is required.
rules.verbatim_quote: Exclusion criteria also apply to patients with previous and recent history of thrombosis (arterial or venous), or the presence of significant risk factors for thrombosis because of the thrombotic risk associated with some therapies (e.g. TPO-R agonists, rituximab and IVIg). In general, a normal clotting screen at baseline will be required.
rules.exceptions: Any deviations may be acceptable if fully justified.

rules.rule_text: Entry platelet count should be less than 30 x 10^9/L. The mean of three baseline platelet counts performed within approximately 14 to 7 days of treatment start is required, and no individual count should be above 35 x 10^9/L.
rules.verbatim_quote: In general the platelet count should be at least < 30 x 10^9/L. The mean of three baseline platelet counts should be performed within approximately 14 to 7 days of the start of treatment, and no individual platelet count should be above 35 x 10^9/L.
rules.exceptions: In specific clinical settings (patients on steroids or with bleeding symptoms) a platelet count < 50 x 10^9/L may be appropriate, with stratification by level of thrombocytopenia recommended.

rules.rule_text: Blood sampling conditions and time allowed between blood sampling and platelet measurement must be specified. All laboratory assessments other than local haematology should be performed in central laboratories.
rules.verbatim_quote: In particular, blood sampling conditions and time allowed between blood sampling and platelet measurement should be specified. All other laboratory assessments should be performed in central laboratories.

rules.rule_text: Previous treatment history including type, dose/schedule, duration, response, and interval since last administration must be documented. Patients must be off prior treatment for a sufficient time to exclude late effects.
rules.verbatim_quote: The type of previous treatment(s), dose/schedule, duration, response (if any), and interval of time since last administered should be documented. Excluding studies which are evaluating an add-on treatment, patients should be off treatment for a time sufficient to exclude a late effect when entering the study.
rules.exceptions: Studies evaluating add-on treatment are excluded from the washout requirement.

rules.rule_text: Studies should enrol both splenectomised and non-splenectomised patients. Patients who relapsed following splenectomy should have an assessment for accessory spleen.
rules.verbatim_quote: Ideally clinical studies should try to enrol splenectomised as well as non-splenectomised patients. Patients who relapsed following an initial response to splenectomy should have an assessment for accessory spleen before entering the studies.

rules.rule_text: Refractory and unresponsive patients should be distinguished and stratified in the clinical study if both groups are enrolled.
rules.verbatim_quote: A distinction between refractory patients and patients unresponsive to one or more agents should be made and if both groups of patients are enrolled in the clinical study stratification is recommended.

rules.rule_text: Concomitant anti-ITP medications are allowed only if on a stable treatment dose/schedule for at least one month prior to enrolment. Concomitant treatment use should be a stratification factor.
rules.verbatim_quote: On entering clinical studies patients may be allowed concomitant specific anti-ITP medications providing they have been on a stable treatment dose/schedule for at least one month prior to enrolment. The use of concomitant treatments should be considered as a stratification factor.

rules.rule_text: Anticoagulants or drugs that affect platelet function such as aspirin or NSAIDs must not be allowed as concomitant medications.
rules.verbatim_quote: Anticoagulants or drugs that affect the platelet function such as aspirin or NSAIDs will not be allowed.

rules.rule_text: A maximum platelet level should be pre-defined as dosing stopping criteria (e.g. blood platelet count > 500 x 10^9/L) that may not correlate to the standard maximum tolerated dose approach.
rules.verbatim_quote: A maximum level should be pre-defined as dosing stopping criteria (e.g. blood platelet count >500 x 10^9/L) that may not correlate to the standard maximum tolerated dose approach.

rules.rule_text: For biological medicinal products, immunogenicity risk must be addressed, particularly potential cross-reactivity of TPO-R agonists with endogenous thrombopoietin.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the case of biological medicinal products the risk for immunogenicity should be addressed, in particular the potential cross-reactivity seen in TPO-R agonists with endogenous thrombopoietin.

rules.rule_text: Population PK studies are recommended to describe PK characteristics and identify potential covariates as predictors of drug exposure. Studies in ITP patients should be stratified on current ITP medication, prior splenectomy, and baseline platelet counts.
rules.verbatim_quote: Additionally, population PK studies are recommended in order to describe the PK characteristics of the drug and to identify potential covariates as predictors of drug exposure. Studies conducted in ITP patients should be stratified on the basis of current ITP medication (if any), prior splenectomy and baseline platelet counts.

rules.rule_text: A PK/PD model is encouraged to describe the time course of drug activity leading to appropriate dosing recommendations. Model-based simulation should determine impact of dose escalation, dose reduction, and dose stopping.
rules.verbatim_quote: the use of a PK/PD model is encouraged to describe the time course of drug activity leading to appropriate dosing recommendations. This model simulation should aim to determine the impact of dose escalation, dose reduction and dose stopping.

rules.rule_text: Dose finding studies must identify a starting dose, response-guided titration guide, and a stopping dose or response. Doses should target a peak platelet count that has doubled from baseline and is within 50-400 x 10^9/L.
rules.verbatim_quote: Dose finding studies are required for new medicinal products with the objective of finding a starting dose, a response-guided titration guide and a stopping dose or response. Doses should be evaluated for a platelet target level and range. A target level of peak platelet count that has doubled from baseline but is within the range of 50-400 x 10^9/L without the need of rescue medication is appropriate.

rules.rule_text: Studies must fully describe dose-dependency for platelet count so a dose titration regimen can be identified, define median time to target level, and explore durability of response. Dose and response stopping criteria must be identified.
rules.verbatim_quote: Studies are required to fully describe dose-dependency for platelet count so a dose titration regimen can be identified. Studies should also define a median time to target level (important in drugs intended for a very quick response) and when possible explore the durability of the response. A dose and response stopping criteria should be identified.

rules.rule_text: A parallel group design including the test drug at one or more doses and one or more control treatments (placebo and/or active comparator) is the appropriate confirmatory study design. A multicentre trial is expected.
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, a parallel group design that includes the test drug at one or more doses, and one or more control treatments (placebo and/or active comparator) is appropriate. A multicentre trial will be expected for accruing sufficient number of subjects.
rules.exceptions: Other study designs may be acceptable depending on the objective of the trial.

rules.rule_text: Randomisation and stratification are required. Stratification factors to be considered include splenectomy status, baseline platelet counts, and use of concomitant ITP treatment.
rules.verbatim_quote: Randomisation will be required and stratification should be conducted taking into account its feasibility and subsequent calculated sample size together with the choice of stratification factors. Stratification factors to be considered may include splenectomy status, baseline platelet counts and the use of concomitant ITP treatment.

rules.rule_text: The non-inferiority margin must be clinically justified when a non-inferiority design is used. Trial characteristics should be the same or very similar to the study in which the active comparator demonstrated efficacy.
rules.verbatim_quote: The choice of non-inferiority margin should be clinically justified (reference is made to Choice of the Non-Inferiority margin - CPMP/EWP/2158/99). the trial characteristics, in particular in the absence of a placebo control, should be the same or very similar to the study characteristics in which the active comparator (in this case the TPO-R agonist) demonstrated efficacy.

rules.rule_text: The primary endpoint should be the increase in blood platelet count (CR or R). Platelet blood count is accepted as a valid surrogate endpoint. The primary endpoint must be well defined in the protocol with justification of clinical relevance.
rules.verbatim_quote: The primary endpoint is expected to be the variable able to provide the most clinically relevant evidence of efficacy related to the primary objective. The platelet blood count is generally used as a valid surrogate endpoint in ITP because it measures treatment activity and is believed to be a reliable predictor of clinical benefit. The increase in blood platelet count (CR or R) should be considered as primary endpoint.
rules.exceptions: A composite or multiple variable may be used as primary endpoint if clinically meaningful and validated.

rules.rule_text: Platelet counts must be confirmed on at least two separate occasions, at least 7 days apart when used to define CR/R, or 1 day apart when used to define NR or loss of response.
rules.verbatim_quote: Platelet counts should be confirmed on at least two separate occasions, at least 7 days apart when used to define CR/R or 1 day apart when used to define NR or loss of response.

rules.rule_text: Complete response (CR) is defined as platelet count >= 100 x 10^9/L and absence of bleeding. Response (R) is platelet count between 30 and 100 x 10^9/L with at least doubling of baseline count and absence of bleeding. No Response (NR) is platelet count < 30 x 10^9/L or less than doubling of baseline count, or bleeding.
rules.verbatim_quote: Complete response (CR): any platelet count >= 100 x 10^9/L and absence of bleeding. Response (R): any platelet count between 30 and 100 x 10^9/L and at least doubling of the baseline count and absence of bleeding. No Response (NR): any platelet count < 30 x 10^9/L or less than doubling of the baseline count or bleeding.

rules.rule_text: The ITP-specific bleeding assessment tool (ITP-BAT) by the International Working Group is recommended for standardised assessment of bleeding.
rules.verbatim_quote: At present no specific tool has been validated but it is recommended to follow the ITP-specific bleeding assessment tool (ITP-BAT) by the International Working Group.

rules.rule_text: Identical between-visit intervals for bleeding assessment must be used for both investigational and comparator arms.
rules.verbatim_quote: For assessment of bleeding it will be required to have identical between-visits intervals for the investigational arm and comparator arm.

rules.rule_text: Corticosteroid dependent or other treatment-dependent patients (excluding experimental drug) will be considered as non-responders.
rules.verbatim_quote: Corticosteroid dependent or other treatment dependent patients excluding the experimental drug will be considered as non-responders.
rules.exceptions: Associated reduced doses or frequency should be recorded as partial effect/activity of the experimental drug.

rules.rule_text: A waiver for children under 1 year of age is applicable. Paediatric studies should use age cohorts: >= 1 to < 6 years, 6 to < 12 years, and 12 to < 18 years.
rules.verbatim_quote: A waiver for children under 1 year of age is applicable. Because of the very low incidence of chronic ITP in those under 3 years of age it is normally acceptable to conduct clinical studies according to the following age cohorts: >= 1 year to < 6 years, 6 years to <12 years, 12 years to < 18 years.

rules.rule_text: Familial inherited thrombocytopenia must be excluded as a diagnosis before entering paediatric clinical studies. ANA screening is required in paediatric ITP due to its association with chronicity.
rules.verbatim_quote: Screening for antinuclear antibodies (ANA) should be conducted because a positive test has been associated with chronicity in childhood ITP. Diagnosis of familial inherited thrombocytopenia should be excluded.

rules.rule_text: Elderly patient enrolment in clinical studies is strongly encouraged, and data on this group would be expected prior to licensing.
rules.verbatim_quote: The enrolment of elderly patients in clinical studies is strongly encouraged and data on this group of patients would be expected prior to licensing.

rules.rule_text: A minimum of 12 months safety data in the target population is expected. Full justification is required if less than 12 months safety data is planned.
rules.verbatim_quote: Due to the chronic nature of this disease a minimum of 12 month data in the target population will be expected but longer periods may be required. A full justification will be required if it is planned to collect less than 12 months safety data.
rules.exceptions: The extent of safety data will ultimately depend on the nature of the study drug and its intended use.

rules.rule_text: Rebound worsened thrombocytopenia (platelet count >= 10 x 10^9/L less than baseline within 30 days of discontinuation) and unacceptably high platelet levels (> 450 x 10^9/L) must be investigated and captured in the safety data.
rules.verbatim_quote: The potential risk for rebound worsened thrombocytopenia following cessation of treatment (platelet count >=10 x 10^9/L less than baseline count within 30 days of discontinuation study treatment) or an unacceptably high blood level of platelets (>450 x 10^9/L) should be investigated. Studies should be designed to capture all such relevant safety data.

rules.rule_text: For TPO-R agonists, bone marrow assessments should be performed at baseline and at justified time points in the pivotal trial, especially for patients on long-term treatment. Assessments should be conducted in central laboratories by an independent expert reviewer.
rules.verbatim_quote: for TPO-R agonists it is recommended to perform bone marrow assessments at baseline and at justified time point(s) in patients included in the pivotal trial(s), especially those patients on long-term treatment. Bone marrow assessments should be conducted in central laboratories by an independent expert reviewer.
rules.exceptions: Bone marrow assessments are not routinely required in clinical studies outside of TPO-R agonist evaluation.
